description: Create src/math_finance_tools package skeleton, Home.py entry point, pages/ directory, plans/ directory. Add streamlit and plotly to pyproject.toml and authorized_libraries.md.
acceptance_criteria: uv run streamlit run Home.py launches with sidebar nav showing both tool pages.
steps: uv add streamlit plotly
Create src/math_finance_tools/__init__.py
Create Home.py with st.navigation pointing to pages/
Create stub pages/1_Debt_Payoff.py and pages/2_Composite_Rate.py
Update authorized_libraries.md with streamlit >= 1.36 and plotly >= 5.20

task: Implement Loan and PayoffResult data models
description: Define Loan dataclass (name, balance, apr, min_payment, intro_apr, intro_end_date, standard_apr), PayoffResult dataclass, and CompoundingMode enum in models.py.
acceptance_criteria: models.py imports cleanly; mypy reports no errors; dataclass fields match all loan input requirements including optional grace period fields.
steps: Create src/math_finance_tools/debt_payoff/__init__.py
Define CompoundingMode enum (MONTHLY, DAILY)
Define Loan dataclass with all fields
Define MonthlySnapshot dataclass (per-loan per-month state)
Define PayoffResult dataclass (total interest, months, snapshots)

task: Implement debt payoff calculator core logic
description: Monthly simulation loop for both snowball and avalanche methods. Handles compounding (monthly and daily), grace period rate switching, minimum payment application, surplus cascade when a payment exceeds remaining balance.
acceptance_criteria: pytest --cov=src reports 100% on calculator.py. Tests cover: single loan payoff, multi-loan snowball vs avalanche ordering, daily vs monthly compounding difference, grace period rate switch at boundary month, surplus cascade when one loan zeroes mid-month, budget equals minimum sum edge case.
steps: Implement apply_compounding() helper (monthly and daily modes)
Implement simulate_payoff(loans, monthly_budget, method, compounding_mode, start_date) -> PayoffResult
Implement surplus cascade loop inside simulate_payoff
Implement grace period rate selection per loan per month
Write test_debt_payoff.py covering all acceptance criteria scenarios

task: Build Debt Payoff Calculator Streamlit UI
description: Streamlit page with dynamic loan row add/remove, budget slider (minimum enforced), compounding toggle, validation error display, comparison table, Plotly line chart, and expandable per-method detail tables.
acceptance_criteria: R1-R12 acceptance criteria pass on manual test. Budget slider minimum is dynamically bound to sum of minimums. Grace period fields appear only when toggle is enabled. Both method traces appear on chart. Tooltip/annotation on method labels.
steps: Build loan input form with st.session_state for dynamic rows
Add grace period toggle per loan row (conditional fields)
Add global: budget slider, compounding toggle
Add validation: budget >= sum(minimums); show st.error and disable button if violated
Wire submit to calculator.simulate_payoff for both methods
Render comparison table via st.dataframe
Render Plotly line chart with one trace per method
Add st.expander for per-method monthly detail table

description: Calculate weighted average APR and monthly interest cost from a list of (balance, rate) pairs.
acceptance_criteria: pytest --cov=src 100% on composite_rate/calculator.py. Tests cover: two accounts equal balance (average is midpoint), one account with 0% rate, all same rate (composite equals that rate), zero total balance edge case returns 0.
steps: Create src/math_finance_tools/composite_rate/__init__.py
Implement calculate_composite_rate(accounts: list[dict]) -> dict with composite_apr, monthly_interest_cost, per_account_breakdown
Write test_composite_rate.py

task: Build Composite Rate Calculator Streamlit UI
description: Streamlit page with dynamic account rows, large composite rate display, monthly cost display, per-account table, and Plotly scatter/bar chart with composite rate reference line and red/green coloring.
acceptance_criteria: R13-R18 acceptance criteria pass. Composite rate updates on every input change. Accounts above composite are red, below are green. Vertical line at composite rate is labeled. Table shows all per-account breakdown columns.
steps: Build account input rows with st.session_state for dynamic add/remove
Wire inputs to calculate_composite_rate on change
Render composite APR in large st.metric
Render monthly interest cost in st.metric
Render per-account table via st.dataframe
Build Plotly chart: APR on x-axis, balance on y-axis, colored by above/below composite, vertical line at composite rate

description: Home page describing the available tools, how to navigate, and brief summary of what each tool does.
acceptance_criteria: App launches to Home.py. Sidebar navigation shows both tools. Home page has a brief description of each tool.
steps: Set page title and layout
Add st.navigation with pages list
Write short tool descriptions with links to each page
